entry.title: Re: Profit Center and Cost Center Groups - Where are they used?
entry.content: Hello, your question has been forwarded to the experts. We hope to provide an answer soon. Kind regards, Jessica

entry.title: Re: ORACLE DATABASE BACKUP
entry.content:
Hi Sai,

It depends on a lot of factors, but you can schedule the most simplest backups via DB13 transaction. It is very straightforward, just double-click on the calendar and choose the backup type you wish to execute (online,offline, complete or full,etc.). You can even choose recurring backup if you wish.

But of course you can run brbackup (the executable of BR*Tools to backup the database) on the command line as well. To do that you have to specify certain arguments to make it work.

Below link contains a documentation that deals with all your upcoming questions:
Backing Up the Database with BR*Tools | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: discount rows in telco accelerator
entry.content:
It seems that price alteration discounts allow customers to see discounts upfront before placing their order. This means that price alterations help customers understand the discounts available for future payments. For example, if you choose to pay monthly, you might get a 10% discount, but if you pay in full now, you could receive a 20% discount. Since this is a future discount, it should not be applied to the cart but instead depend on the selected payment option.

You can read more in the following documentation:

  • Price Alterations (Discounts) 
  • Global Discounts 
  • Price Alterations (Discounts) - Model Details 

P.S. I've never used the Telco and Utilities Accelerator, but I tried to summarize the information I found in the official documentation. I hope this helps you investigate further in your codebase.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance plan was not scheduled - Error
entry.content:
Hi Priya,

Time gap was given for each measurement document. Scheduled error is not coming now.

Thanks!

entry.title: why does Fiori LeaveRequets -Onbehalf function converts status "Posted" to "Informed"?
entry.content:
Dear all,

with the Fiori app "My leave requests -Onbehalf funtion" I am facing a problem. As a Employee/Leave-Requester I see the leave requests with correct status as in SENT, APPROVED or REJECTED. But after running RPTARQPOST, my manager /approver sees the "POSTED" status entry as "INFORMED", as an Employees (Leave requester) I see as the status as "APPROVED".
Is there any Settings that i can change so the approver also sees Status as "APPROVED" or "POSTED" instead of "INFORMED"

I am very new to SAP and Fiori.If am posting this Question in wrong place , please do tell me where i can post for better reach.

Any help would be much appreciated .

Thanks in Advance.

NewBee

entry.title: Re: S4H Fiori Launchpad Message - Password login no longe...
entry.content: Thanks Gregor.

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2502 - Available Now!
entry.content:
Today, January 31st, 2025, SAP Integrated Business Planning 2502 was generally released as planned.

After the successful launch of the early adopter program, we’re excited to announce that Joule in SAP IBP is now generally available.

In addition to the very first GenAI use case that enables on-the-spot access to assistance and enablement content, we also offer document grounding with Joule. Document grounding allows customers to use their own specific unstructured or semi-structured documents to provide answers to user questions in Joule. Customers can connect their document repositories via a Microsoft SharePoint site. Check out the Getting Started Guide for Document Grounding and the SAP IBP 2408 Joule demo video.

Note that the use of Joule requires a separate license. Region restrictions may apply. Please check the contractual details with your account manager and see the Joule Onboarding Guide.

Now let’s explore some of the key features of the first SAP IBP release in 2025:

Foundation

Experience Up to 40% Faster Data Integration with SAP IBP's Cutting-Edge Parallel Processing

In today's fast-paced digital world, efficient data integration is crucial for businesses to stay competitive. SAP IBP's cutting-edge parallel processing technology offers a significant boost, potentially speeding up data integration by up to 40%. This innovative approach allows for simultaneous processing of master and transactional data loads, provided there are no dependencies. Customers with global planning operations and tight batch windows, especially those with multiple divisions and high data frequency, will find this particularly beneficial as it enables seamlessly synchronized decisions based on real-time information, providing superior results.

Best of all, this feature will be automatically enabled for customers, eliminating the need for any additional effort or configuration steps. Overall, customers will experience enhanced performance, reduced bottlenecks, and a significantly more efficient data integration process, driving their business success forward.

Response and Supply

Manage Complex Supply Chain Relations with Real-time Integration of Storage Location MRP Areas

We now offer an out-of-the-box integration of MRP areas from your SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA backend system to SAP IBP. In the ERP system, an MRP area is an organizational unit within a plant comprising one or more storage locations for which you carry out separate material requirements planning and inventory management. With these storage location MRP areas, you can execute material requirements planning at a more granular level than at the plant level.

You can use real-time integration (RTI) to transfer selected storage location MRP areas to SAP IBP, where they are mapped automatically as independent locations, allowing you to manage complex supply chain relations within a plant without creating additional plants in your ERP backend system.

Demand

Fast and Easy Insights into Selected Forecast Algorithm ​

The winning forecast algorithm of best-fit forecasting can now be stored in a dedicated attribute for further use: Planners can display the forecast algorithm as an additional column directly in the planning UI, they can run statistics on winning forecasting algorithms​, and use the attribute as a filter within planning and analytics use cases​.

With this simplified setup, it's much easier to fine-tune your forecasting and get better insights and usage statistics.

Planning UIs

Mastering Advanced Contextual Navigations in Planner Workspaces

By enabling the use of master data to look up filter values, contextual navigations were further enriched in release 2502. 

Additionally, planners can now also define in which context navigations to another web application or to the SAP GUI show up. For example, you can define that they are only relevant in the context of a specific planning view.  

Best Practices/Analytics Stories

New Preconfigured Analytics Story and Planner Workspace for MRO Planning

​​With SAP IBP 2502, additional Best Practices content for IBP for MRO enables fast adoption of best-in-class analytics ​for the Maintenance & Operations Planning (M&OP) process.

With the new preconfigured analytics story and Planner Workspace, you can instantly analyze the results of the M&OP process steps in an aggregated and detailed format in the web user interface.​ The content enables data-driven decision-making during the MRO planning processes. ​

​The new analytics story for MRO includes visualizations on key measures such as forecast, parts segmentation results, inventory levels, resource utilization. ​

The new Planner Workspace for MRO covers the detailed level of all relevant input and output generated during the M&OP process.

More Information

  • SAP IBP What’s New Webinars 2502
  • 2502 What’s New: Help Portal Documentation
  • What’s New in Best Practices
  • SAP IBP Community Page
  • SAP IBP Roadmap
  • SAP Customer Influence Portal for SAP IBP
  • Questions? Ask here

entry.title: How to Use JWT Cookies for Authentication in SAPUI5 with OData V2/V4 (CAPM(node.js) in backend
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am working on a SAPUI5 frontend with a CAPM (Node.js) backend and have implemented a custom authentication mechanism using JWT tokens. Here’s my setup:

  • My CAPM backend initially had an unauthenticated service but later, I added JWT-based authentication.
  • Upon successful OTP verification, I inject a JWT token as an HTTP-only cookie in the response.
  • In the frontend, I am using SAPUI5 with OData V2 and V4 services.
  • I have also implemented Bearer authentication but now I am facing issues in using the JWT cookie for authenticated requests in the frontend.

My Questions:

  1. How can I use the JWT cookie in SAPUI5 when making OData V2 and OData V4 (both) requests?
  2. How do I correctly set the headers or configure authentication in SAPUI5 to use the JWT token from cookies?

Since the token is stored in an HTTP-only cookie, I cannot directly access it in JavaScript. I would really appreciate any guidance on handling authentication with JWT in SAPUI5 when using OData services.

I have also attached a sapui5 odatav4 call in which i have tried to add header in the odatav4 call but unfortunately it didn't worked ! 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to use I_ProductTP_2 in mock-framework?
entry.content:
1. You can't use the CDS Test Environment with I_ProductTP_2 because some of the CDSs it is based upon are not released.
  2. What does "test the CDS" mean? To me as "can only use released stuff" what's the actual difference in using the different frameworks? From a data-mocking-framework I expect that it behaves consistently as if I were interacting with the database. CDS Test Environment does that for my own CDSs, SQL Test Environment does not seem to work like that (changes on I_ProductTP_2, which is built upon I_Product, do not affect I_Product and vice-versa)

I have not seen any explanation on this anywhere yet, google was no help neither, so can you point that out somehow?

entry.title: Re: Find out if a component of a structure is a structure or a table type
entry.content: Check DESCRIBE FIELD ... TYPE (where type"h" = table and type "u/v" is structure) for example or explore RTTS in general.

entry.title: Supplier determination for inbound deliveries from production order
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing the issue that inbound deliveries for a production order are not getting created automatically after confirming the production order. I identified that the issue is based on the fact that no vendor can be determined for that delivery.

Would someone be so kind to link me the IMG-path for determining the supplier for inbound deliveries from production?

Thanks and best regards,

Jonas

entry.title: Re: discount rows in telco accelerator
entry.content:
Hello, thanks for your response. I understand what you're saying, but what I want is to set up a discount that is not for future use. For example, you can buy a modem, a router, and make the payment at checkout. What you're mentioning makes sense for subscriptions, like when you sign up for an internet connection or a TV channel subscription.

I will keep researching.

Best regards.

entry.title: SAP BTP Hackathon at SAPHILA 2025 (AFSUG Members)
entry.content:
This year we launched the first-ever AFSUG and SAP collaboration SAP BTP Hackathon.

In a nutshell:

  • All AFSUG members can nominate a business use case that showcases SAP BTP capabilities
  • Up to 25 customers will be selected to build out their use case and compete for a chance to demo it live on stage at SAPHILA 2025
  • 8 finalists will participate in the first-ever SAPHILA Demo Jam in June for a chance to win some cool prizes

When talking to Amanda Gibbs, the CEO of the African SAP User Group (AFSUG), she shared her thoughts:

"The AFSUG SAP BTP Hackathon is an extraordinary opportunity for our members to showcase their creativity, problem-solving skills, and innovative thinking. By collaborating with SAP on the SAPHILA Hackathon, we’re creating a platform where customers and partners can explore the transformative potential of SAP BTP, turning bold ideas into tangible solutions that deliver real business value. At AFSUG, we believe in the power of community and shared learning. This hackathon is not just about competition; it’s about fostering connections, gaining insights, and driving meaningful change together. I encourage all our members to seize this chance to innovate, learn from SAP’s global experts, and be part of something truly impactful."

I really can’t beat that intro, so I am not even going to try 🙂

 

The Details

SAP BTP is THE platform that brings together application development, automation, data management, analytics and planning, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for integrating and extending SAP applications.

It’s used by customers, partners and SAP to innovate and optimize business processes while leveraging the latest and greatest cloud technology.

The SAP BTP hackathon challenge will showcase how customers can use SAP BTP to adapt and optimize their business processes. But that’s not all!

AFSUG members will also benefit from in-depth enablement sessions led by SAP product experts to help them through the ideation and build phases.

The participating customers, those who have had their use case selected for the SAP BTP hackathon, will also have access to expert coaches during their build phase. Coaches are there to make sure “no use case is left behind” 😉

Once the build phase is completed, customers will pitch their use case to a jury of SAP experts. The jury will choose 8 finalists based on different criteria, like business relevance, SAP BTP usage, innovation and vision, that will go on to demo their use case in front of a live audience at SAPHILA 2025.

To show off your efforts, participating customers will get special badges added to their SAP Community profile. So, if you are not yet an SAP Community member, what are you waiting for?

 

To All AFSUG Members

Don’t miss this unique opportunity to participate in the SAP BTP hackathon!

In short:

  1. Submit your use case here by February 18
  2. (Selected customers) Build your prototype by April 22
  3. Get ready to pitch your demo from May 2 to May 16
  4. (Finalists) Compete in the Demo Jam at SAPHILA 2025 on June 3

In case you still need convincing, let me reiterate some of the perks:

  • Attend SAP BTP enablement sessions led by SAP experts for inspiration and knowledge.
  • Engage with the SAP ecosystem and get special badges to display on your profile. Don’t forget to create an account in the SAP Community
  • Get direct access to SAP BTP coaches to support you during your build phase.
  • Expand your SAP BTP knowledge and bridge the gap between business and IT.

 

Conclusion

We are beyond excited to see what our customers come up and see their ideas come to life with SAP Business Technology Platform.

And yes, we would love to bring this format to other user groups and locations. One step at a time 😉

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2022 FPS1 Fully-Activated Appliance standard installation
entry.content:
Hi,

If we want to install in sap host(say hdbs4hj2e) both ABAP and JAVA with different SID(S4H for ABAP and J2E for Java) then the same document will work??. Still can we proceed with the same steps described in this document? Please suggest.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM : DIALOG RESPONSE TIME MONITORING
entry.content:
Hi, 

I contacted SAP asking for this, the reply was:

"Dear Customer, 

Thank you for your patience with the processing of this case. 

I have checked this internally with our Developer and we can confirm that it is not possible to collect only single task types in Real user monitoring as it is not possible to determine the outgoing calls in advance. "

SAP as usual, new monitoring tool and does not monitor properly one of the most important items to monitor

entry.title: Unlocking the True Potential of Value Management and Adoption in HR
entry.content:
How Embracing Value Management Can Transform Your HR Initiatives and Drive Organizational Success

In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, the role of Human Resources has transcended traditional boundaries. No longer confined to administrative tasks, HR has become a strategic partner that drives organizational growth and innovation. By leveraging cutting-edge technology, businesses can amplify their impact. But how do we ensure that our HR initiatives are not just activities, but value-driven endeavors that propel our organizations forward?

The answer lies in Value Management.

What Is Value Management?

Value management is a systematic approach to maximizing the benefits of a project or initiative by aligning it with the organization's strategic objectives. It's about ensuring every action taken delivers optimal value, driving both efficiency and effectiveness.

In the context of HR and SAP SuccessFactors, value management involves:

  • Aligning HR strategies with business goals

  • Measuring the impact of HR initiatives on organizational performance

  • Continuously optimizing processes for better outcomes

Why Value Management Matters in HR

1. Bridging the Gap Between HR and Business Strategy

Too often, HR initiatives operate in silos, disconnected from the broader business objectives. Value management compels us to ask crucial questions:

  • How does this initiative support our organizational goals?

  • What measurable value does it bring?

By aligning HR projects with strategic objectives, it ensures efforts contribute directly to the company's success.

2. Demonstrating Tangible ROI and Beyond

In an era of tight budgets and increased scrutiny, demonstrating the return on investment (ROI) of HR initiatives is essential. However, it's important to recognize both quantitative and qualitative benefits when assessing ROI.

  • Quantitative Benefits: These are measurable in numerical terms, such as cost savings, increased revenue, or reduced time-to-hire. They provide hard data that can be readily analyzed.

  • Qualitative Benefits: These include improvements in employee satisfaction, brand reputation, or organizational culture. While harder to measure, they are equally important and can lead to significant long-term gains.

Understanding the difference between ROI and qualitative and quantitative benefits allows our customers to capture the full spectrum of value that HR initiatives can deliver. By acknowledging both, our customers can build a more comprehensive business case and demonstrate the multifaceted impact of their efforts.

3. Driving Continuous Improvement with Practical KPIs

Value management isn't a one-time exercise but a continuous process of evaluation and optimization. It encourages a culture of ongoing improvement, where feedback loops and data-driven insights lead to better decision-making.

To start effectively, focus on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) that are easy to measure and demonstrate value without requiring extensive data collection. This approach allows for quick wins and builds momentum for broader value management initiatives.

  • Examples of Practical KPIs:

    • Employee turnover rate

    • Time-to-fill positions

    • Training completion rates

    • Employee engagement scores

By selecting KPIs that are straightforward and require minimal data points, you can swiftly showcase the impact of your HR initiatives and make a compelling case for further investment.

Implementing Value Management with SAP SuccessFactors

1. Set Clear, Measurable Objectives

Begin with the end in mind. Define what success looks like for your initiative.

  • Example: Reduce employee turnover by 15% over the next year.

Consider both quantitative and qualitative objectives to capture the full range of potential benefits.

2. Align Initiatives with Business Goals

Ensure your objectives support broader organizational aims.

  • Example: Reducing turnover contributes to cost savings (quantitative) and improves team morale (qualitative), enhancing overall productivity.

3. Leverage Analytics for Insight

Use SAP SuccessFactors' robust analytics tools to track progress and uncover insights.

  • Key Metrics to Monitor:

    • Turnover rates

    • Time-to-fill positions

    • Employee engagement scores

    • Performance metrics

4. Start with Simple, Impactful KPIs

Select KPIs that are easy to measure and clearly demonstrate value. This approach allows you to quickly illustrate the effectiveness of your initiatives without getting bogged down in data complexity.

  • Why This Matters:

    • Builds confidence in your value management approach

    • Provides immediate feedback for adjustments

    • Lays the foundation for incorporating more complex KPIs later

5. Engage Stakeholders

Involve key stakeholders from the outset to build support and gather diverse perspectives.

  • Who to Involve:

    • Executive leadership

    • Steercom Committee – Budget Owners

    • Process Owners

    • Department heads

    • Employees impacted by the initiative

6. Iterate and Optimize

Regularly review your progress and be prepared to adjust your approach.

  • Questions to Consider:

    • Are we on track to meet our objectives?

    • What obstacles have emerged?

    • How can we address them effectively?

Challenges and How to Overcome Them

1. Resistance to Change

Change can be unsettling. Overcome this by communicating openly about the benefits and involving employees in the process.

2. Data Silos and Complexity

Data scattered across systems hinders insight. Start with KPIs that require fewer data points to demonstrate value quickly. As your value management practice matures, you can incorporate more complex data sources.

3. Lack of Clear Metrics

Ambiguous goals lead to ambiguous results. Invest time in defining clear, quantifiable metrics, including both quantitative and qualitative benefits.

Real-World Success Story

Company XYZ: Faced with declining employee engagement, Company XYZ implemented a value management approach using SAP SuccessFactors.

  • Actions Taken:

    • Conducted employee surveys to identify pain points (qualitative data)

    • Aligned initiatives with the goal of increasing engagement scores by 20%

    • Implemented targeted development programs

  • Results:

    • Achieved a 25% increase in engagement scores

    • Saw a 10% reduction in turnover (quantitative benefit)

    • Improved overall productivity and enhanced company culture (qualitative benefit)

The Future of HR Lies in Value Creation

As HR leaders, embracing value management empowers you to be proactive leaders within your organizations. By focusing on both quantitative and qualitative outcomes and strategic alignment, you can elevate the impact of your work.

Starting with practical, easy-to-measure KPIs allows you to demonstrate value quickly and build a strong foundation for more advanced initiatives. Remember, every step towards effective value management brings you closer to unlocking the true potential of your HR strategies.

Join the Conversation

What are your experiences with value management in HR? Have you found certain KPIs more effective in demonstrating value? How can you strategically align HR KPIs with business goals to craft a compelling transformational narrative? Share your thoughts, and let's explore how we can collectively drive greater value through our HR initiatives.

Join our monthly run practical Value sessions. 

Click to review global calendar for H1_2025 

 

About the Author

Nicole Wright is a passionate HR professional specializing in leveraging technology to drive strategic value. With over 25 years of experience in SAP HR On Premise and SuccessFactors, Nicole is dedicated to helping organizations unlock the full potential of their human capital.

@darrenfry

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content:
Hi Fatima,

Thanks for the blog.

while uploading the SearchCodeTool.zip file in integration suite, getting below error.

Could you please check and update the files in GitHub repository ?

entry.title: Re: PaymentPurpose does not appear for Payment Medium: JP_ZENGINKYO_I_XML
entry.content: Informed expert.

entry.title: Re: discount rows in telco accelerator
entry.content: The attribute "popAlteration" (Product Offering Price Alteration) is optional, so leaving it empty should work for you requirement. Have you tried it?

entry.title: Re: Implementing 1SG – Group Reporting: Missing Clear Setup Instructions
entry.content: Informed expert.

entry.title: Re: discount rows in telco accelerator
entry.content:
Hello, it's theoretically optional, but if you try to create a DiscountRow in the back office, it requires you to fill it in. Once created, you can update it and leave it empty, but in that case, when adding it to the cart, the following exception occurs:

INFO [hybrisHTTP8] [AbstractRestHandlerExceptionResolver] Translating exception [java.lang.NullPointerException]: Cannot invoke "de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.model.TmaDiscountProdOfferPriceAlterationModel.getPriceEvent()" because "productOfferingPrice" is null ERROR [hybrisHTTP8] [AbstractRestHandlerExceptionResolver] java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.model.TmaDiscountProdOfferPriceAlterationModel.getPriceEvent()" because "productOfferingPrice" is null at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.impl.TmaAbstractOrderDiscountAlterationPriceBuilder.buildPrice(TmaAbstractOrderDiscountAlterationPriceBuilder.java:46) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPopBuilder.getGlobalAlterationsFromPOPDiscounts(DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPopBuilder.java:232) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPopBuilder.populatePriceAlterations(DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPopBuilder.java:197) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.impl.TmaAbstractOrderOneTimeChargeComponentPriceBuilder.buildPrice(TmaAbstractOrderOneTimeChargeComponentPriceBuilder.java:76) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.impl.TmaAbstractOrderOneTimeChargeComponentPriceBuilder.buildPrice(TmaAbstractOrderOneTimeChargeComponentPriceBuilder.java:29) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.impl.TmaAbstractOrderProductOfferingPriceBuilder.createPrices(TmaAbstractOrderProductOfferingPriceBuilder.java:50) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.builder.orderentry.DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPriceBuilder.buildPrices(DefaultTmaAbstractOrderEntryPriceBuilder.java:46) at de.hybris.platform.b2ctelcoservices.pricing.strategy.impl.DefaultTmaFindAbstractOrderEntryPriceStrategy.lambda$findBasePrice$0(DefaultTmaFindAbstractOrderEntryPriceStrategy.java:76)

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Cancellation document pending – Cannot release via VFX3
entry.content:
Cancel the S1 with an S2 is only applicable in some excpetional cases - see: Solved: Cancellation of cancelled invoice (SAP note 388489... - SAP Community

Further, did you check with your Tax department if an S1 cancellation of a commercial invoice towards customer is even allowed? Normally you should issue a credit memo and not cancel an invoice.

If the reason the S1 is not posted to accounting is related to the period being closed, just adjust the invoice date to be in the current (open) period.

entry.title: Curricula Completion
entry.content:
Hi,

We've imported a bunch of items (+learning history) and need to place in them a curricula. As the items (and curricula) have been completed on the legacy system is there a way to manually complete the curricula on SF LMS?

Thanks,
Syd

entry.title: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for RESTful WebServices
entry.content:
Hi guys,

after updating from 4.3 SP02 P8 to 4.3 SP04 P10 we are facing logon issues on the BI Launchpad:

We have a second server which was not updated yet. I compared all config files - no differences.

In the Webapp_BIlaunchpad_trace files I see he following error:
No logon token provided in the X-SAP-LogonToken HTTP header or query parameter. (RWS 00008)

I'm running out of ideas. Anybody facing the same issue?

Thanks
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Predictive Quantity Contract Consumption
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

I found the solution. I had to run the report MM_PUR_PAI_CTR_PREDICT_UPDATE in TCODE SE38. 

It works now. 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: FBL*H transactions - OOM Dump(STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG) and analysis
entry.content: Check also SAP hints for performance described in KBA 2000002 - FAQ: SAP HANA SQL Optimization.

entry.title: Deep creation of Product via I_PRODUCTTP_2 does not work
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to create a new, basic product via the RAP-BO I_PRODUCTTP_2.
I filled all reqired fields and, as the desciption i a required field, too, I tried to create deep the description, using the association _productdescription.

In no constellation the modify / commit is successful.
Now I get the error:
Property PRODUCT is a key and cannot be initial CMD_PROD_RAP(003)
But: I provided the root entity with this content and I cannot provide it to the sub-node as it is inherited from the root node.
Is there a way to get beyound this?

I'm using an OnPrem 758 system SAP S/4HANA 2023 SP01 (02/2024) FPS system
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA(productid) = '000000000053009920'.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_producttp_2
ENTITY product
CREATE FIELDS ( product baseunit productgroup laboratoryordesignoffice crossplantstatus
weightunit producttype industrysector ) WITH VALUE #( ( %cid = 'prd1'
product = productid
baseunit = 'KG'
productgroup = '5121'
laboratoryordesignoffice = 'AS1'
crossplantstatus = 'E0'
weightunit = 'KG'
producttype = 'HALB'
industrysector = 'M' ) )
CREATE BY \_productdescription
FIELDS ( productdescription language )
WITH VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'prd1'
%target = VALUE #( ( %cid = 'prddescr1'
product = productid
productdescription = 'My Description'
language = 'E' ) ) ) )
FAILED FINAL(failed) ##NEEDED
REPORTED FINAL(reported) ##NEEDED.

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on pa...
entry.content: @TharinduWdid you seriously think that I haven't try that code? That didn't work for me. There is only difference I wrote these annotations in projection view not in metadata extension maybe that is cause that it didn't work. And again I want to emphasize that value help didn't work for new record on the screen where record is created. I'll check your solution, but I don't believe that it works.

entry.title: Re: How to display an attribute of a class, which belongs...
entry.content:
For instance attributes, you need the instance of the class to access an attribute. In my opinion, accessing it using only the name of the class and attribute does not work.
For static attributes, you can write <class name>=><attribute name>.

entry.title: Re: The Beginner’s Guide to SAP Activate – Best Practices, Guided Configuration and SAP Activate Met
entry.content: Good One

entry.title: Re: Controlling the number of times reminders are send for Order acknowledgment
entry.content: We request that reminders be sent to vendors by email based on the PO creation date. (EKKO-BEDAT) Three reminders will be sent: 1. The first reminder will be sent 1 day after the creation date. 2. The second reminder will be sent 5 days after the creation date. 3. The third reminder will be sent 10 days after the creation date. Reminders should stop if a value is present (not null) in either the “Order Ack” field or in a line with a delivery date in the PO “Confirmations” tab,

entry.title: Re: Can change documents be written when using the ADDR_C...
entry.content: You could use ADRESSE_WRITE_DOCUMENT[2] (Look with transaction SCDO for object ADRESSE[2])

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities: Status Update January 2025
entry.content:
The year 2025 has begun, and it is time again for an update on our SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities. We had started this session in July 2023 as our single channel for collecting your suggestions for improvements related to SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

For more details, read my previous blog post New SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

As of 30 January 2025, we have received a total of 87 improvement requests (IR) in our session:

17 IRs have already been delivered since the session started. A complete list, starting with those IRs that have received most votes is shown below:

  • IR 308111 - EPRODCUST: extension of IS-U master data templates 
  • IR 321271 - BAPI_ISUPROFILE_IMPORT: EDM Import Application log  
  • IR 304589 - Full Device Replacement Reversal Process (new transaction) 
  • IR 313649 - Profilevalue status handling in formula variables 
  • IR 314904 - Change documents for EDM formula allocation 
  • IR 308110 - Mark/identify service providers as obsolete/unusable 
  • IR 331220 - IS-U billing: change the determined finale billing date 
  • IR 310707 - During device installation: automatically create MR documents for existing, upcoming bil... 
  • IR 310314 - Enable the Meter Reading Result to use the GOS (Object MTRREADDOC) 
  • IR 308652 - ICWebClient: change of IS-U contracts in web surface 
  • IR 304691 - Record the requirement for value replacement for all EDM imports 
  • IR 325261 - Include C/O and Address unique identifier in SAP ISU 
  • IR 307543 - Request Test Mode for BAPI_ISUPROFILE_IMPORT 
  • IR 307337 - Regenerate load profiles despite exitsting billings 
  • IR 306201 - SAP t-code EA22 to view Bill documents from archival call 
  • IR 304698 - Definition of profile value statuses eligible for value replacement 
  • IR 315621 - System-Id and Client in Fiori Launchpad Title

You can also view the current list of all improvement requests delivered within the SAP Continuous Influence sesison for SAP S/4HANA Utilities under https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3504/ideas-completed.

3 IRs has been accepted for development and is planned to be delivered in Q1-Q2/2025:

  • IR 338268 - EA40: no customer screen for CI fields of table ERDK 
  • IR 307906 - Fixed address is saved in CAA2 despite it is deleting in the same time in transaction BP
  • IR 338301 - AMI Services: possibility to change the proxy to EOIO queue 

2 IRs have the status "Planned (Long-Term)", which means they have been identified as being a larger investment and have been incorporated into the product roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

  • IR 317134 - EEDM02 transaction in Fiori
  • IR 328753 - Deleting device info records en masse

2 IRs are currently under review, and 1 IR has status "Need More Info".

15 IRs have the status "For Long-term Consideration", and most of them are requesting new RAP interfaces for different entities of the SAP S/4HANA Utilities data model. 

9 IRs have the status „Acknowledged”, 5 of them have 7 or more votes and are close to being reviewed (10 votes required to do so).

37 IRs have the status “Not planned”, and we had to reject most of them either because they did not receive enough votes within twelve months or because they are out of scope for the session SAP S/4HANA Utilities. We would like to remind you that within this session dedicated to the utilities industry, we can only acknowledge and accept such IRs that are related to the utilities-industry specific part of SAP S/4HANA. For further options for influencing SAP products (beyond SAP S/4HANA Utilities) please see How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. A comprehensive list of all active SAP Customer Influence sessions is available here: SAP Continuous Influence Sessions - Overview.

A big thank you to all improvement request owners and other contributors for their active participation in our SAP Continuous Influence Session so far! Please continue to do so – by submitting your own ideas or by voting for improvement requests already submitted by your peers. Remember that such improvement requests that have not reached the required threshold of 10 votes within 12 months will be rejected automatically. Please be encouraged to validate and prioritize the important ones from your point of view.

Please note that a valid S user is required to participate in our (and other) SAP Continuous Influence session(s). For details see knowledge base article 3014377 - Accessing the Continuous Influence Session "SAP S/4HANA Utilities".

We appreciate your input that helps us to continuously improve SAP S/4HANA Utilities according to your business requirements.

entry.title: Re: Setting the deletion flag for Real Estate Management...
entry.content:
Hi Yuliia,

Please check if below indicator is set for object type I5.

The you should have the Deletion indication available.

Thank you,

Achyuth reddy K

entry.title: Re: How to use I_ProductTP_2 in mock-framework?
entry.content:
With "test the CDS" I mean that you have developed a new CDS view and want to write a test for the logic in it. For I_ProductTP_2, this would be done by a team responsible for the product master in SAP product engineering. If you create your own CDS view, you perhaps want to use the CDS Test Double Framework to test the behavior of your view.
So in the CDS TDF, you mock the dependent-on components (DOC) of the CDS view. The CDS view is the code under test (CUT). In the OSQL TDF, the database artifact (CDS view, table,...) is the DOC and your code that's using the DOC is the CUT.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/unit-testing-with-abap-unit and the help nodes below it provide some help with writing tests in ABAP. In the "Dependency Management" node of the tree on the linked page you can explore the the different mocking frameworks.

entry.title: How can I build a sum over an associated table in SAP CAP?
entry.content:
Hi, I'm trying to sum over an amount in an associated table
My entity definition looks as following: 

entity Trips as select from t.Trips as t left join t.Receipts as r on r.trip.tripId = t.tripId  {
  t.*,
  sum(r.amount) as sum_amount: Decimal
}
order by t.tripIdasc;

 However, if I access the defined entity via the exposed OData service my sum_amount is always either null or "0.00". 

My schema looks as following:

entity Trips: managed {
    key tripId                  : Integer     
                                @title: '{i18n>schema.reiseid}'
                                @ui.Hidden;     
    receipts                    : Association to many Receipts on receipts.trip = $self
                                @ui.Hidden;   
}

entity Receipts {
    key receiptId               : Integer
                                @ui.Hidden;     
    key receiptLineId           : Integer
                                @ui.Hidden;  
    amount                      : Decimal(10, 2)
                                @readonly
                                @Measures.ISOCurrency: 'EUR';
}

I also receive an error in the console (I omitted a few columns from the select):

[SqlError: sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near ")": line 1 col 1344 (at pos 1344)] {
  code: 257,
  sqlState: 'HY000',
  level: 1,
  position: 1344,
  query: 'SELECT tripId AS "tripId", sum_amount AS "sum_amount", null AS "IsActiveEntity", HasActiveEntity AS "HasActiveEntity", null AS "HasDraftEntity", DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID AS "DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID" FROM TravelCockpitService_Trips_drafts ALIAS_1 WHERE ( ) IN ( ( ) , ( ) , ( ) , ( ) , ( ) , ( ) , ( ) ) ORDER BY EXT_REISE_ID ASC'
}

entry.title: Re: How To Read Data From TCP/IP Socket and Port Using ABAP Program
entry.content: Not a good idea to have images as hyperlinks, people have to click each image individually and display them outside the question). Instead of uploading the image files, please embed the images inside your question (copy/paste).

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned on Tuesday, March 11th in Bengaluru, India 🇮🇳 from 9:00 to 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen from SAP.

The event is kindly being hosted by TATA Consultancy Services(TCS). Thank you! Please note that spots are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. 

Location : TCS Victor building; ITPL Main Rd, Pattandur Agrahara, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: OData Exploration with EPM Tutorial - ES5
entry.content: So, you're talking about https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/odata-02-exploration-epm..html

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Email Attachment Mail Adapter
entry.content:
Hi Supreet,

I usually use the method "message.addAttachmentObject" to add attachments to the mail. Here you have more information: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/adding-cloud-integration-attachments-in-code/ba-p/13327199

After that you have to check the flag "Add Message Attachment" in the Mail adapter and automatically all the attachments are added to the mail. Take in account the mime types supported: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-mail-receiver-adapter

Hope it helps.

Regards,

Roberto

In WCOCO I don't see any option for the message determination, how can I trigger a print?

entry.title: Re: Delete the whole planning category in SAP S4/HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Dear @JanaHriv,

there is a check which prevents users from deleting plan categories which already contain data.
If you want to delete a category which is already in use, you need to delete the plan data first.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules (5/5)
entry.content: @ArunK2 - Responsibility Management as of now doesn't support custom workflow.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM / Manage S/4H Cloud implementation for...
entry.content:
Hi Manuela,

it depends a bit on what you want to share across the companies. Are they using the different processes or are these separate or parallel roll outs of the same processes? 

e.g. you can have a scope (e.g. finance) which has country tags and you use the releases to roll it out to the different companies. For company specific documentation or test cases you can also use tags or naming conventions.

please also have a look into the best practice guide: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/alm/sap-cloud-alm-for-implementation/Implement-S4HANA-Cloud-Public-Edition.pdf

we can also have a chat on this: since.seidler@sap.com

Cheers

Since

entry.title: Re: Deep creation of Product via I_PRODUCTTP_2 does not work
entry.content:
Hi All,

via debugging of the exeption and the stack, startin with cm_cmd_prod_message->new and above I came to the solution.

It was as simple as:
CREATE BY \_productdescription
FIELDS ( productdescription language )
WITH VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'prd1'
                             Product = productid  "THIS LINE IS REALLY IMPORTANT
                             %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = 'prddescr1'
                                                                product = productid

Even if it works now, the call of the EML is really tricky.
Even if is syntactically correct, the successs of the command is absolutely not guaranteed.


Best regards 

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on pa...
entry.content: Well I don't know you so don't really what you've tried either 😄 I've deployed this on production so works great for me. Like I said the one thing I can't do is have the value help accept parameters. As soon as that's done, my odata v4 RAP service is generatee for odata v4.01 which isn't supported by Fiori Elements. But as far as passing parent entity's fields into the value help of a child entity it works. Note that in my additional binding, the local element accesses the parent element through the association-to-parent. It works in both create, update and draft modes for me. I am on S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 On Premise.

entry.title: Re: Portuguese National Budget for 2025- Payroll Legal Changes
entry.content:
Hi Anabela,

I guess I already understood!

The percentage related to the year was aplied to the amount of income, and not to the amount of the exemption.

So in this case you have to compare the tax if it was normal IRS contribuition or IRS Jovem contribuition, right? We don't use the limit of 55 IAS. Correct?

In this case what should be the value of the wagetype IJOV?

Thanks,

Liliana Ferreira

entry.title: Re: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for...
entry.content:
There are many reasons for it

It could be the wrong configuration of the RESTful Access URL,

1 Check the access URL of the RESTful service under CMC-> Applications-> RESTful Web Services
2. If you use Load Balancer, check that you use the correct RESTful URL
3. Be aware that a self -signed certificate is no longer supported
4. TLS 1.3 is currently not supported and TLS 1.2 should be set to be used exclusively in Tomcat configuration

entry.title: Re: CALM & MS Teams Integration changes due to MS O365 co...
entry.content: Many thanks to Dirk (DiakSima) for giving me some further input on how to improve the graphic, and make it easier to read! Now the image uses different shapes to help visually contrast Workflows and Webhooks. The sequence in the image has also been updated so all 3 webhooks (I, II, and III) are grouped together vertically, instead of being split up like in the original image.

entry.title: Re: How to use I_ProductTP_2 in mock-framework?
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

if you want to use the entity for update you have to CREATE it first.
See an example for creation in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/deep-creation-of-product-via-i-producttp-2-does-not-work/qaq-p/14002462#M4901394

The mixture of sql and cds mocking in AUnit is really tricky.

Have fun

Andreas

entry.title: what is service request for system refresh on sap portal
entry.content: Help me with the SR name for system refresh on sap portal, for system refresh activity on Rise project

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content:
Dear Fatima,

It’s always exciting to see open-source solutions that help streamline development processes—especially within the SAP ecosystem. The code examples and step-by-step guidance you provided offer invaluable insights for fellow developers looking to enhance their integration workflows. Kudos to you for sharing such a well-structured and helpful resource!

In Acorel, We have created a specialized tool to address the impending deprecation of the ${in.body} statement or any other mass changes. Specifically, our solution does not only automate the task of scanning but also creates backups, replaces outdated references with the new ${body} notation, redeploys flows, and provides a comprehensive report—all to ensure a safe and efficient transition. And this proved to be successful in some clients already.

It is great that all these tools or flows help clients to incorporate any changes smoothly, especially for the changes that has to be implemented in multiple scenarios!

Best regards,

Oguzhan

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Bangalore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

You'll become acquainted with how embedded Machine Learning in SAP HANA can be used to build ML models. In working through this CodeJam you'll also become familiar with how the SAP Business Application Studio can be used to work with Jupyter notebooks.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Monday, March 10th in Bangalore, India from 09:00 to 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Vitaliy Rudnytskiy from SAP.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/hana-ml-py-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

The event is being hosted by Tata Consultancy Services. Thank you!

Location:

TCS Victor building; ITPL Main Rd, Pattandur Agrahara, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066

Note that spaces are limited: we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

I always use and highly recommend a different user other than SYSTEM on source.
In this way, SYSTEM can stay locked and secured (as recommended by SAP.)

Hello, 

I'm trying to establish a connection between SAP CPI to Microsoft 365 with OAuth 2 credentials using Mail sender adapter, although my credentials are deployed successfully, when I'm trying to do the connectivity test, I'm getting (javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: AUTHENTICATE failed). 

Below are the screenshots attached for reference

Connectivity Test

 

Credentials deployed

I've given scope as:

https://outlook.office.com/IMAP.AccessAsUser.All 
https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send 

offline_access

 Am I missing something? Please help me out.

Thanks in advance

Shreyas

entry.content: Hi, What role collections are assigned to the user trying to select the "scenario" value in the project? Are any other fields in the project creation screen greyed out as well, or is it only the "scenario" one? Thanks and regards, John

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM / Manage S/4H Cloud implementation for...
entry.content: Hi Since, Thank you so much for your reply! That's already helpful. I will reach out to you for more details. Many THANKS, Manuela

entry.title: Re: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for...
entry.content:
Many change between SP02 and SP04, so that many existing/new configurations should be adapted. For example, you can use the relative URL path for a RESTful URL

TLS 1.2 should be set exclusively in the Tomcat configuration (Tomcat use TLS 1.3 by default)

Another tip: After the upgrade, do not forget to remove the content of the Tomcat work directory.

entry.title: Re: How to show a busy indicator in story while the table...
entry.content: Thank you for your wonderful reply.

entry.title: Re: How to change the year in number ranges with nr status
entry.content: It means it already exists. Leave FBN1 and try again. NB: you may indicate the year 9999 to avoid creating an interval each year.

entry.title: Re: Siemens Team Center integration with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi Ayse,
there are multiple options for connecting custom data with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence depending on the technology used by SIEMENS Team Center:

  • Ingestion API to push CSV-based data files 
  • depending on the data persistency of SIEMENS Teamcenter, you can use one of our connectors to connect directly to the database 
  • you can extract the data to a data warehouse/integration solution like SAP Datasphere, Snowflake and others
  • you can use a middleware like SAP Integration Suite to fetch data and push it to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 

Best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Integration between Cloud ALM and non-SAP architectur...
entry.content:
Hi Bruno,

Currently SAP Cloud ALM doesn't offer "out-of-the-box" integration with non-SAP deployment tools. If you want to request that kind of "out-of-the-box" integration, please create a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Customer Influence Campaign.

However, hypothetically it may be possible to have a kind of very simple integration (triggering deployments) by leveraging the functionality of SAP Cloud ALM External API Management.

Our product offers a Resource Changes API for Features. SAP Cloud ALM generates an "event" each time a feature is created, updated, or deleted. The "event" includes a payload with data from the Feature. This includes the status of the Feature. If the non-SAP deployment tool has its own API for triggering deployments, it may be possible to set up an integration so that the tool triggers a deployment activity. For example, a Feature reaches status "Ready for Production", the event is generated and the external API for the non-SAP deployment tool is called to trigger some deployment activity.

However, this approach would be very restricted. The API currently does not provide a means for the Feature to receive data about any actions taken externally. So you would not be able to programmatically have deployment artifact IDs in the Feature, or see the status of the actual deployment. You would also need to try and find a way to identify the correct artifact to deploy for the Feature. This may be possible if the artifact has some kind of ID from the Feature (e.g. the artifact ID is the same as the Feature "externalId" (e.g. "5-539", etc.). Or you could leverage some kind of middleware to perform pre-processing before the request reaches the non-SAP deployment tool. But if not, this hypothesis may not work at all. 

If you would prefer a less effort intensive alternative to my hypothesis, then creating a request in the Customer Influence campaign is the best way to proceed. 

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: How to filter a bar graph by dimensions from the dropdown option via scripting?
entry.content:
Hi,

Here is the scenario. I have created a bar graph and user wants to filter this graph by either of the three dimension (Brand, Region, Company Code). I know we can create a standard dimension input control which will serve this requirement but due to some constraint I cannot use the dimension input control. Is there a way, I can create a dropdown and filter the bar graph using the values from this dropdown via scripting. I'm looking at other places also but mostly people are using the dropdown to filter by measures instead of dimension.

Anyone who has faced this similar issue or knows the workaround in this scenario. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

entry.title: How to read table data associated with archive object CRM_IBASE?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

    I am trying to archive ibase related data in Sap CRM.

    For this I have used the archive object CRM_IBASE.

    This archive object has many tables like IBIB, IBIB, IBST etc,

    I have used tocode SARA to archive one ibase data. It successfully completed with green color satus and delete completed. 

    However if I search for my ibase archived data in the ZARIXCR29 table i don't find my data. Why is this so? My requirement is to read the archived data and put that into a excel file.

For this i have used the code as below:

SELECT SINGLE * FROM admi_files INTO wa_admi_files WHERE document = docn.

SELECT * FROM zarixcr29 INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_archivekeys
             WHERE archivekey = wa_admi_files-archiv_key.

LOOP AT lt_archivekeys INTO la_archivekeys.

*Read information from archive
  CALL FUNCTION 'ARCHIVE_READ_OBJECT'
    EXPORTING
      object         = arobj  "'CRM_IBASE' 
      archivkey      = la_archivekeys-archivekey
      offset         = la_archivekeys-archiveofs
    IMPORTING
      archive_handle = ls_handle
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS         = 1.

CALL FUNCTION 'ARCHIVE_GET_TABLE'
      EXPORTING
        archive_handle          = ls_handle
        record_structure        = 'IBIB' "'IBIB'
        all_records_of_object   = 'X'
        automatic_conversion    = 'X'
      TABLES
        table                   = lt_ibib "lt_ibib "Header data
      EXCEPTIONS
        end_of_object           = 1
        internal_error          = 2
        wrong_access_to_archive = 3
        OTHERS                  = 4.

ENDLOOP.

  However i am not getting any data from any tables like IBIB or IBIN, etc using the FM ARCHIVE_GET_TABLE. What am i doing wrong? 

  What i need to do to read the table data? 

  Please help!

Thanks

Gopal

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps: Inline Frame remove sandbox attribute
entry.content:
I found a solution for my use-case 🙂 

For everybody who is interessted, I did it by using an iframe and using pdf.js to render the pdf by myself: https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js

Configuration in SAP Build Apps:

Use component inline frame:

  • set values in the sandbox field.
    • For my use case "allow-scripts" (needed to use javascript) is everything I need
  • In the srcdoc value you can paste your complete html file, which can also include a script to render the pdf

 

My html file:

<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pdf.js/2.10.377/pdf.min.js"></script>
<html>
	<body>
		<div >
			<p style="max-width: fit-content; margin: auto;"></p>
			<canvas id="pdfCanvas" style="border-style: solid; border-width: 2px;"></canvas>
			<div style="max-width: fit-content; margin: auto; margin-top: 20px;">
				<button id="btnPrevious" style="width: 100px;">Previous</button>
				<button id="btnNext" style="width: 100px;">Next</button>
			</div>
		</div>
		<script>
			var pdf;
			var pageNr = 1;
			var maxPages = 0;
			var url= "<yourURL>/example.pdf";

			window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);

			function init() {
				document.getElementById('pdfCanvas').addEventListener('click', nextPage);
				document.getElementById('btnNext').addEventListener('click', nextPage);
				document.getElementById('btnPrevious').addEventListener('click', previousPage);
			}

			function nextPage() {
				if(pageNr >= maxPages) {
					throw new Error(`Reached end of pdf file (Site ${pageNr}/${maxPages})`);
				}
				else {
					pageNr++;
					renderPage(pdf);
				}
			}

			function previousPage() {
				if (pageNr == 1) {
					throw new Error(`Reached beginning of pdf file`);
				}
				else {
					pageNr--;
					renderPage(pdf);
				}
			}
			
			pdfjsLib.getDocument(url).promise.then((pdf) => {
				this.pdf = pdf;
				maxPages = pdf.numPages;

				console.log(maxPages);
				renderPage(pdf)
			});

			function renderPage(pdf) {
				pdf.getPage(pageNr).then((page) => {
					var scale = 1;
					var viewport = page.getViewport({ scale: scale });

					var canvas = document.getElementById('pdfCanvas');
					var context = canvas.getContext('2d');

					canvas.width = viewport.width;
					canvas.height = viewport.height;

					var renderContext = {
						canvasContext: context,
						viewport: viewport
					};
					page.render(renderContext);
				}).catch((err) => {
					console.log('ERROR: ' + err);
				});
			}
		</script>
	</body>
</html>

 The width/height can changed set by using the scale value.

It is also possible to create a pdf from base64. For that have a look here: https://mozilla.github.io/pdf.js/examples/#:~:text=Hello%20World%20using%20base64%20encoded%20PDF

entry.title: Re: PAN number field is missing in Business Partner API S...

Hi @EC,

I found a solution. Have a look here: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/sap-build-apps-inline-frame-remove-sandbox-attribute/td-p/14001301

You can also render the pdf from base64. So eventually it helps you

entry.title: Process the DCD for released sales orders.
entry.content:
Remove the blocked sales order which is already released from the documented credit decision list.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Process the DCD for released sales orders.
entry.content: Please refer the SAP KBA ##3101401 - Releasing the documented credit decision are not transferred.  https://me.sap.com/notes/3101401

entry.title: Re: Creating SPRO Node for Flight View Clusters in SAP
entry.content: Thank you @Khan-Muskan for sharing, very helpful

entry.title: Re: Custom Cards in Project Overview
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation doesn't offer the ability to create custom cards or reports. Instead, we offer a suite of pre-built cards and reports which have filtering options.

Your query is "how to create a report to show Open Tasks by Users". That data can be shown by accessing the Task Distribution child app of the Analytics app. The Analytics app can be accessed in the Implementation tab of SAP Cloud ALM. There are also other useful Task related child apps, like Task Trend Analysis.

Please note, these apps offer no export/output option for their graphics.. They are designed to be used as part of the project management workflow within SAP Cloud ALM itself. Currently if you need to extract a graphic from the apps to use elsewhere, you will need to take a screen shot. If you wish to request this functionality, please create a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Customer Influence Campaign.

If you wish to use other tools/software to create custom dashboards or reports (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud), SAP Cloud ALM offers several Pull APIs so that data can be extracted for this purpose. The full list of Pull APIs are documented in the API Guide for SAP Cloud ALM section API Reference. This includes a Task API.

Thanks and regards,
John

entry.title: Re: Metadata not updating after change in behavior projec...
entry.content:
Hi,

I thought this information could be useful for others in the future. The issue was in the solution was deactivating the metadata in SPRO in:

Navigation: ABAP Platform -> SAP Gateway -> OData Channel -> Administration -> Cache Settings -> Activate or Deactivate Cache in ABAP Platform -> SAP Gateway -> OData Channel -> Administration -> Cache Settings -> Activate or Deactivate cache

and deactivating the job /UI5/UPD_ODATA_METADATA_CACHE

entry.title: Re: Feature Control in RAP, Static Feature Control in RAP, Part - 1
entry.content: Thank you @Krishna_karale

entry.title: Re: Implementing 1SG – Group Reporting: Missing Clear Set...
entry.content:
Hi guys,

This is a complex and huge topic I have to say.

1SG is a complex process in FI and its logic are not easy to understand. If you are the first time to check this business, an consulting help in this area will be very helpful.

1. Is there a clear, structured document or guide on setting up 1SG from scratch and testing if it works correctly? A step-by-step explanation covering both configuration and validation would be extremely helpful. 

Sorry we have only TEST script in 1SG best practice. I don't have this even in SAP side. I want to get this guide like you ....to be honestly.

In SAP learning hub, if you search 1SG or Group reporting, consolidation, you will found some learning course, which will be helpful. Besides, you can check below links

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-consolidation-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-group-reporting

Performing Consolidation with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Group reporting

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/introduce-sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-group-reporting

Of course , the most valid resource is our help portal Group Reporting | SAP Help Portal

2.  What is the correct sequence for setting up 1SG? There are multiple moving parts, like ACDOCA data extraction, ACDOCU mappings, eliminations, and reporting. Is there a defined process to follow?

I guess you want to know which task should be used in Data/Consolidation Monitor.  You can define this based on 1SG best practice. It's enough for most cases.  Of course, you can define it based on your demand.

 

3. How does the data flow from ACDOCA to ACDOCU in this process? What are the key configuration steps to ensure data is transferred correctly?

Release universal journal task in Data monitor app. 

You can check help for this information and we have a lot points about this area. I guess the key steps is about GRPL. Consolidation version and Consolidation Unit, FS item.

4. How are intercompany eliminations handled? For example, if Company A sells to Company B within the group, how does SAP ensure revenue is not double-counted in the consolidated financial statements?

This part is about Reclassification. You can check help for further.

5. Is it possible to renumber the SAP pre-delivered FS (Financial Statement) Items? If yes, what is the correct approach?

NO, you can change the setup for FS item and create your new FS item, but you cannot renumber pre-delivered FS item

6. Are there any additional KBAs, SAP Learning Hub materials, or best practices that provide better clarity on 1SG implementation?

I have provided some resources in above answers. Besides, you can found more in SAP Community other channel. 

We have some KBAs in SAP FOR Me and you can search them.

It's not easy to use Group Reporting and the information is very limited in Cloud product. We are trying to make it more rich and better but it's difficult. For on premise product side, we have similar structure, so you can check op resource if necessary. 

Hope this could help you solve the concern.

BR

Jerry

entry.title: UPDATE PAN ON VENDOR MASTER DATA
entry.content:
We are using API to create business partner 'API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner' in the role of supplier fin-accounting . 

The field J_1IPANNO is not available on the API. 

As the integration is flowing online , how can we update this field in the supplier vendor master data on the tab Supplier : country -specific enhancement 

is there any badi, enhancement for public cloud ? 

Thanks 

Ana

entry.title: Re: Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info S...
entry.content:
the problem occures when "NLS_CHARACTERSET" and 'NLS_NCHAR_CHARACTERSET' for your CMS DB are set to other than AL16UTF16 or AL32UTF8.

So you should check the CMS -DB for which "NLS_CHARACTERSET" and 'NLS_NCHAR_CHARACTERSET' are supported

see KBA 3437779 - Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info Store error while creating Users/Groups, issues with all authentication methods (WinAD, LDAP, SAP) BI 4.3SP04

entry.title: Re: 2502 C4C Release Discussion
entry.content: We are happy to share the release briefing content of our SAP Sales and Service Cloud (aka Cloud for Customer) for the 2502 (February 2025) release: https://static.hybris.com/audience_engagement/customer_newsletter/c4c/2025/2502_Roll_Out_SAP_Cloud_for_Customer_Consolidated.pdf
